behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic on template text/rules, not module handoff. Behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow. Integration was considered, but disqualified because no required real harness assembly or cross-module state is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the linter a prompt-template fixture that includes a phase instruction snippet copied from SKILL.md and assert it returns a violation with the expected location/rule id; feed a clean template and assert pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixtures + assembler). Behavior explicitly requires "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness," so Unit is too low. No real network/subprocess or model-output dependence is stated, which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-assembly pipeline with representative fixtures, then assert the final prompt text has no phase-instruction leakage patterns from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is a wiring/contract test across invocation, file loader, and prompt construction. Unit is too low because the failure is "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" vs hard-coded rules. Higher levels were considered, but behavior text does not require live model judgment; it focuses on context source correctness, disqualifying Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation with real plugin/skill file fixtures and assert loaded context originates from those files (not embedded phase rules), e.g., by checking resolved context payload/provenance markers.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is harness configuration and file-variant substitution across modules. Unit is too low because this is execution-path wiring. No requirement for real OS/network/subprocess beyond local test files, and no requirement yet that model output be correct, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the harness twice with identical user prompt text: once with normal SKILL.md and once with degraded/missing variant via fixture switching; assert prompt text input is unchanged and runs complete with the selected variant.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill context degrades. A stubbed model could always pass/fail deterministically and miss this regression, so Integration would hide the real failure. Workflow was considered, but behavior describes one prompt-response behavioral check, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test with fixed user-style prompt against baseline skill and degraded-skill variants; assert baseline passes and degraded variant fails on the same structural criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering/handoff between model-call module, artifact writer, and parser. Unit is too low because sequence across modules matters. System was considered, but behavior text only requires diagnostic artifact capture (can be local in-process file write); no real external infra condition is specified. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because content quality is not under test.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke harness with a controlled model response path, then assert diagnostic artifact is written with raw output bytes/text and timestamp before parser/extractor execution (verified via call order/log markers).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is assertion-rule logic itself (schema path/field-placement checks), a local function/object boundary. Integration was considered, but disqualified because behavior text does not require harness wiring or live model execution. No OS/network/subprocess or model nondeterminism is required.
behaviors.test_description: Given crafted outputs, verify assertion functions fail when required schema paths/role fields are misplaced even if keywords are present, and pass when structure is correct.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says "Under live model execution," and success depends on real model outputs exhibiting structural variation/regression. Mocked outputs could hide this, so Integration is too low. Workflow was considered, but the behavior states a single live execution assertion comparison, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests on prompts known to produce occasional keyword-present but structurally invalid outputs; assert structural checks fail where keyword-only checks would pass, demonstrating real-regression detection.
